deals.name: Stitch AI
deals.description: Open-core agentic eval platform that lets engineering teams diff LLM behavior across model versions in CI.

deals.name: Helix Bio
deals.description: Generative protein design platform for antibody discovery, training a foundation model on cryo-EM data.

deals.name: Tessera Robotics
deals.description: Bimanual manipulation robots for SMB manufacturing — software-first stack with rapid teach-by-demo.

deals.name: PorterRoad
deals.description: Software for the cold-chain trucking long tail — broker-neutral telematics + load matching.

deals.name: Morwenna
deals.description: Tidal stream power generators built from rotating-mass turbines, targeting 24/7 baseload behind the meter.

deals.name: Kindly Labs
deals.description: Voice-first agent for behavioral-health intake calls — replaces front-desk triage at outpatient clinics.

theses.thesisOneLiner: LLM apps are software, and software needs CI.
theses.beliefStatement: As model upgrades become the dominant source of regressions, eval-as-code in CI becomes a non-negotiable layer of the LLM dev stack — owned by infra, not data science.
theses.wedge: Diff-style eval reports inside GitHub PRs.
theses.whyNow: Frontier models ship every 6 weeks; teams have no rollback playbook.

newsItems.title: Stitch raises $22M Series A from a16z

theses.thesisOneLiner: Generative biology becomes IP-defensible only with proprietary wet-lab data.
theses.beliefStatement: The next defensible AI bio companies own a closed-loop between in-silico design and in-house wet-lab validation; pure compute plays will commoditize.
theses.wedge: Cryo-EM data flywheel paired with generative model.
theses.whyNow: Cryo-EM throughput just 10x'd; sequence-only models are plateauing.

theses.thesisOneLiner: Foundation-model robotics wins the SMB long tail before factories.
theses.beliefStatement: The first durable robotics businesses will sell to thousands of SMBs (job shops, kit packers) where teach-by-demo dramatically outperforms hard-coded automation, not to lighthouse F500s.
theses.whyNow: Open-source manipulation policies (RT-X, Pi 0.5) hit production-ready in 2026.

newsItems.title: Tessera demos at SPC Fellowship Demo Day

theses.thesisOneLiner: Software eats the long tail of US trucking — starting with cold chain.
theses.beliefStatement: Tier-2/3 cold-chain truckers are the last unsoftwared piece of US logistics. A vertical SaaS that bundles telematics + broker-neutral load matching can compound margin away from incumbents.
theses.wedge: Free telematics dongle bundled with margin-taking marketplace.
theses.whyNow: FMCSA ELD mandate 2.0 forces the long tail to digitize in 2026.

theses.thesisOneLiner: Tidal stream is the only renewable that's both 24/7 and forecastable.
theses.beliefStatement: Once tidal stream LCOE clears the threshold of $80/MWh, behind-the-meter island and coastal industrial loads become a step-function market that solar/wind cannot serve.
theses.wedge: Rotating-mass turbines that survive in shallow estuaries.
theses.whyNow: First UK contracts-for-difference auction including tidal closes Q4 2026.

theses.thesisOneLiner: Behavioral health intake is the wedge for healthcare voice agents.
theses.beliefStatement: Behavioral-health clinics have the worst phone-tree problem in healthcare; voice agents that get 80% of intake right unlock real ARR per clinic and bypass EHR integration headaches.
theses.wedge: DEA/HIPAA-compliant voice stack pre-sold to outpatient psych networks.
theses.whyNow: Telehealth parity sunset means clinics need higher throughput per FTE.

counterparts.name: Snapeval
counterparts.description: GitHub-native LLM eval suite for backend engineers.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Same wedge (CI-first eval), but a non-US team going after the EU compliance angle — Stitch isn't there yet.

counterparts.name: Promptkit
counterparts.description: Open-source eval harness with hosted runs.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Adjacent — open-source flywheel matters; a16z thesis tilts CI-side, this is more notebook-first.

counterparts.name: Cryolab AI
counterparts.description: Cryo-EM data co-op + generative antibody design.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Mirrors the closed-loop wet-lab + generative thesis exactly, but at seed and with a data-co-op moat angle.

counterparts.name: Antifold
counterparts.description: Open-source antibody design models with paid hosted inference.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Sequence-only model — close to Helix's red flag, but team has wet-lab access via partnership.
counterparts.llmRedFlag: No in-house wet lab — lukewarm on differentiation.

counterparts.name: Polyarm
counterparts.description: Dual-arm cell sold as monthly leasing to job shops.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Same SMB-first hypothesis, leasing GTM resonates with capital-constrained shops.

counterparts.name: Loophand
counterparts.description: Teach-by-demo controllers retrofitting existing UR cobots.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Retrofit angle is even more capital-light; thesis-aligned.

counterparts.name: Chillship
counterparts.description: Free telematics for refrigerated trucks; revenue from broker spread.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Same exact wedge as PorterRoad. Cheaper and earlier; deserves a real cheque.

counterparts.name: Boxhaul
counterparts.description: Asset-light dry-van brokerage with AI dispatch.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Adjacent but dry-van not cold-chain; misses the wedge specificity.

counterparts.name: Tideturbine
counterparts.description: Estuary-class tidal stream generators for behind-the-meter loads.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Same target market and estuary thesis, with a behind-the-meter pilot already signed.

counterparts.name: Solera Health
counterparts.description: Voice agent for psych intake — DEA-compliant audit trail.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Same intake wedge with a stronger compliance angle for controlled-substance prescribers.

counterparts.name: Calmwave
counterparts.description: Voice AI scheduling for teletherapy clinics.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Adjacent — scheduling, not full intake. Lower clinical risk but smaller wedge.

counterparts.name: Trustlens
counterparts.description: Eval and red-teaming for production agentic apps.
counterparts.thesisRationale: Production-side angle complements Stitch's CI-side; thesis still aligned.

byThesisCluster.thesisLabel: LLM apps are software, and software needs CI.
Generative biology becomes IP-defensible only with proprietary wet-lab data.
Foundation-model robotics wins the SMB long tail before factories.
Software eats the long tail of US trucking — starting with cold chain.
Tidal stream is the only renewable that's both 24/7 and forecastable.
Behavioral health intake is the wedge for healthcare voice agents.

reasoning.rationale: Enrich shows hires from GitLab CI + Datadog observability. Combined with the round investor pattern (a16z + Lux), the implicit bet is on an infra-side LLM eval product, not consumer prompts.
Cryo-EM job postings + ARCH on the cap table point to a wet-lab-first positioning. Sequence-only models are flagged as anti-thesis.
SPC fellowship cohort + Lux co-investor. Headcount mostly applied robotics, no humanoid signals → SMB-first thesis.
Open-source eval harness with CI-first positioning out of Berlin. Tiny team but exact wedge match. Strongest counterpart for Stitch.
Data-co-op moat is a credible variation on Helix's wet-lab thesis; seed cheque size lets us mirror conviction at 1/4 the valuation.
Sequence-only — flagged as anti-thesis per Helix's red-flag list. Kept on watchlist only.
Anika Patel: cryo-EM PhD + DeepMind protein team. Marco Russo: prior bio-AI exit to Recursion. Repeat-operator + domain-expert pairing — top decile founder team for this thesis.
Lena Brandt previously sold a DevTools co. to GitLab and personally built CI flake detection — directly the wedge. Repeat operator with deep domain context.
